name: Frankie Trumbauer
abstract: Orie Frank Trumbauer (May 30, 1901 – June 11, 1956) was an American jazz saxophonist of the 1920s and 1930s. His main instrument was the C melody saxophone, a now-uncommon instrument between an alto and tenor saxophone in size and pitch.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I was sort of after the sound of Frankie Trumbauer at that time... I wanted to sound like, I guess, Frankie Trumbauer... The first solo that I copied... was Frankie Trumbauer's 'Singing the Blues'

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: He wrote "Crazy Cat" and "Three Blind Mice" in collaboration with Trumbauer.

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Frankie -- with Frankie before I played with Eddie Hayward

about_quotes.speaker: Flip Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: he was the first guy that influenced me, that made something with me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Flip Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: I think we were both influenced by Frank Trumbauer, pretty much.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: in the band were Jack Teagarden, Charlie Teagarden, his brother, a marvelous trumpet player, let's see, Frank Trumbauer, C-melody saxophone. I am trying to think, Carl Kress, I played tenor, Carl Kress guitar
